role_summary: Civil rights attorney whose Appalachian Research and Defense Fund of Kentucky carried civil rights legal work into Appalachian poverty-law contexts; co-subject of a joint oral history with his wife Jean Rosenberg (catalog entry 61).
ai_reading: John Rosenberg shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 61 (catalog slug 'john-and-jean-rosenberg'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: John Rosenberg is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 61 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for John Rosenberg; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Independent biographical sourcing for John Rosenberg is pending.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 61
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)
